message:
fix(installer): handle LTS-only upgrade settings for interim releases

Ubuntu LTS releases ship with Prompt=lts in /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades,
which prevents do-release-upgrade from seeing interim releases (24.10, 25.04, 25.10).
ACFS targets Ubuntu 25.10, requiring a multi-hop upgrade path through these versions.

Problem:
  - User starts on Ubuntu 22.04 LTS or 24.04 LTS
  - do-release-upgrade only offers the next LTS (24.04→none or 22.04→24.04)
  - ACFS expected 24.04→24.10 but got "no upgrade available" or wrong target
  - Upgrade would fail silently or skip to wrong version

Solution (3 coordinated changes):

1. install.sh - Export target version environment variables
   - Sets UBUNTU_TARGET_VERSION and UBUNTU_TARGET_VERSION_NUM
   - Ensures ubuntu_upgrade.sh knows the actual target (not just defaults)

2. ubuntu_upgrade.sh - Validate upgrade target and enable interim releases
   - ubuntu_do_upgrade() now accepts expected_next_version parameter
   - Validates detected upgrade matches expectation before proceeding
   - Provides clear error message if Prompt=lts is blocking:
     "Unexpected upgrade target detected. Fix: set Prompt=normal..."
   - Calls ubuntu_enable_normal_releases() at start of upgrade sequence
   - Passes expected target through the upgrade chain

3. upgrade_resume.sh - Mirror fixes for post-reboot resume flow
   - Also calls ubuntu_enable_normal_releases() after reboot
   - Calls ubuntu_restore_lts_only() after final upgrade completes
   - Calls upgrade_remove_motd() to clean up status banner
   - Passes expected version to ubuntu_do_upgrade()

Upgrade path now works correctly:
  22.04 → 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10

Each hop validates the target before running do-release-upgrade, and
interim releases are explicitly enabled before the upgrade sequence begins.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1424,6 +1424,10 @@ run_ubuntu_upgrade_phase() {
     target_minor="${TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION#*.}"
     target_version_num=$(printf "%d%02d" "$target_major" "$target_minor")
 
+    # Ensure ubuntu_upgrade.sh uses the requested target (not just its defaults).
+    export UBUNTU_TARGET_VERSION="$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION"
+    export UBUNTU_TARGET_VERSION_NUM="$target_version_num"
+
     # Check if upgrade is needed (using numeric comparison)
     if ubuntu_version_gte "$current_version_num" "$target_version_num"; then
         log_detail "Ubuntu $current_version_str meets target ($TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION)"

@@ -457,14 +457,25 @@ ubuntu_prepare_upgrade() {
 # Perform single-version Ubuntu upgrade (non-interactive)
 # Returns: 0 on success (reboot may be required), 1 on failure
 ubuntu_do_upgrade() {
-    local next_version
-    next_version=$(ubuntu_get_next_upgrade)
+    local expected_next_version="${1:-}"
+
+    local next_version=""
+    next_version="$(ubuntu_get_next_upgrade 2>/dev/null || true)"
 
     if [[ -z "$next_version" ]]; then
         log_error "No upgrade available"
         return 1
     fi
 
+    if [[ -n "$expected_next_version" ]] && [[ "$next_version" != "$expected_next_version" ]]; then
+        log_error "Unexpected upgrade target detected"
+        log_error "  Expected: $expected_next_version"
+        log_error "  Detected:  $next_version"
+        log_error "This is usually caused by LTS-only upgrade settings (Prompt=lts)."
+        log_error "Fix: set Prompt=normal in /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades and retry."
+        return 1
+    fi
+
     log_section "Upgrading Ubuntu to $next_version"
     log_warn "This will take 30-60 minutes. System will reboot when complete."
 
@@ -967,6 +978,10 @@ ubuntu_start_upgrade_sequence() {
     log_step "Current: Ubuntu $current_version"
     log_step "Target:  Ubuntu $UBUNTU_TARGET_VERSION"
 
+    # Ensure non-LTS upgrades are permitted (LTS defaults to Prompt=lts).
+    # ACFS targets interim releases (e.g. 24.10, 25.04, 25.10) on the path to 25.10.
+    ubuntu_enable_normal_releases || true
+
     # Calculate upgrade path
     local upgrade_path
     upgrade_path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path)
@@ -999,7 +1014,7 @@ ubuntu_start_upgrade_sequence() {
 
     state_upgrade_start "$current_version" "$first_target"
 
-    if ! ubuntu_do_upgrade; then
+    if ! ubuntu_do_upgrade "$first_target"; then
         log_error "First upgrade failed"
         state_upgrade_set_error "do-release-upgrade failed"
         return 1

@@ -76,6 +76,9 @@ fi
 # Set state file location for resume context
 export ACFS_STATE_FILE="${ACFS_RESUME_DIR}/state.json"
 
+# Ensure non-LTS upgrades are permitted (LTS defaults to Prompt=lts).
+ubuntu_enable_normal_releases || true
+
 # Mark that we've successfully resumed after reboot
 log "Marking upgrade as resumed"
 state_upgrade_resumed
@@ -94,6 +97,12 @@ if state_upgrade_is_complete; then
     # Mark upgrade as fully completed
     state_upgrade_mark_complete
 
+    # Restore LTS-only upgrade settings if we changed them.
+    ubuntu_restore_lts_only || true
+
+    # Remove upgrade MOTD banner (so users aren't told upgrade is still running).
+    upgrade_remove_motd || true
+
     # Cleanup systemd service and temporary files
     log "Cleaning up resume infrastructure..."
     ubuntu_cleanup_resume "acfs-upgrade-resume"
@@ -143,7 +152,7 @@ log "Starting upgrade from $current_version to $next_version"
 state_upgrade_start "$current_version" "$next_version"
 
 # Perform the upgrade
-if ! ubuntu_do_upgrade; then
+if ! ubuntu_do_upgrade "$next_version"; then
     log_error "do-release-upgrade failed"
     state_upgrade_set_error "do-release-upgrade failed for $current_version → $next_version"
     upgrade_update_motd "UPGRADE FAILED - check logs"
